products_reviewed: Cloudflare AI Gateway and MCP server portals
HCP Terraform policy enforcement / Sentinel / OPA
unsupported_spira_claims: SPIRA is the only pre-action agent gate
SPIRA governs arbitrary tool calls today
SPIRA governs arbitrary MCP/API/database actions today
SPIRA replaces IAM or identity systems
SPIRA is a universal AI-agent governance platform
SPIRA formally proves all parsers or upstream producers

supported_spira_differentiators.claim: SPIRA currently positions credibly as an artifact-backed evidence gate for supported software artifacts.
SPIRA's accepted positioning is useful because it separates authorization policy from evidence justification.

The reviewed sources did not document the full SPIRA combination of domain-specific artifact evidence, deterministic machine contract, explicit not_evaluated, explicit not_claimed, evidence/proof references, and model non-authority in one system.

partially_supported_differentiators.claim: SPIRA is differentiated from model guardrail systems.
partially_supported_differentiators.limitation: True for reviewed content/evaluation guardrails, but not a claim that SPIRA is broader or more mature.

partially_supported_differentiators.claim: SPIRA is differentiated from infrastructure policy gates.
partially_supported_differentiators.limitation: Only partially true: OPA, HCP Terraform/Sentinel and Spacelift strongly overlap with Terraform-plan policy gating. SPIRA is differentiated by machine-contract fields and AI-agent model non-authority, not by the existence of Terraform pre-apply gating.

rejected_differentiators.claim: No competitor exists in pre-action agent governance.
rejected_differentiators.reason: APort/OAP and Microsoft AGT explicitly address pre-action/tool-call/agent governance layers.

rejected_differentiators.claim: No competitor evaluates Terraform plans before action.
rejected_differentiators.reason: OPA, HCP Terraform/Sentinel, Spacelift and Conftest document Terraform/config policy workflows.

rejected_differentiators.claim: SPIRA can be pitched as arbitrary action authorization today.
rejected_differentiators.reason: Accepted SPIRA positioning does not include arbitrary tool/MCP/API/database action adapters.

recommended_positioning_wording: SPIRA is an artifact-backed evidence-to-contract gate for supported AI-assisted software actions. Policy says what an agent is allowed to do; SPIRA checks whether supported evidence justifies doing it now.
